commands.review_summary:
This PR updates CodexBar install documentation, localized website copy, the Homebrew release playbook, and changelog credit to describe the official Homebrew cask as universal on Intel and Apple silicon.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a runtime bug; the stale-docs mismatch is source-verifiable because current main says Homebrew is Apple Silicon-only while the current cask and release metadata use the universal app archive without an architecture restriction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Owner-authored docs PR should stay open for maintainer merge/check handling; there is no narrow code repair for automation.

target.title: docs: clarify universal Homebrew cask
target.body:
Closes #1193

## Summary

- Describe the official Homebrew cask as universal on Intel and Apple silicon.
- Update the English website fallback and all 21 localized `hero.fineprint` / `detail.installBody` messages.
- Remove the stale arm64-only instruction from the Homebrew release playbook.
- Add changelog credit for the original Intel support report.

## Proof

Exact candidate: `3954d1ee`

- Current official cask v0.36.1 downloads `CodexBar-macos-universal-#{version}.zip`, requires macOS Sonoma, and has no architecture restriction.
- `node Scripts/check-site-locales.mjs` passes after the final current-main rebase: 21 locales, 50 site messages.
- `make docs-list` passes after the final current-main rebase.
- `make check` passes after the final current-main rebase with zero SwiftFormat or SwiftLint findings.
- Exact prior site head autoreview reported no actionable findings, confidence 0.90; site content is unchanged by the rebase.
- Existing-profile Chrome visual smoke passes for English, German, and RTL Arabic: updated hero/install copy renders without clipping; provider grid, install cards, and RTL layout remain intact.
target.author: steipete
